evals.name: image positive classic model
evals.prompt: Use the oiticica-image skill. Review this English-classic model: Dickens comparing a legal fog to the actual fog around Chancery so image and institution meet. Explain why the passage should be preserved rather than flattened.
evals.expected_output: The response preserves a strong classic model and judges it by the skill's objective rubric.
assertions: The output identifies the relevant Oiticica concept.
The output says the model should be preserved or treated as strong.
The output applies at least two objective rubric checks instead of generic praise.

evals.name: image negative classic contrast
evals.prompt: Use the oiticica-image skill. Review this weak English-classic-style passage: Hope was a lighthouse, a sword, a garden, and a river in his heart. Give one concrete contrast with Weak, Fault, Better, Why, and Rubric.
evals.expected_output: The response gives a concrete Oiticica contrast and fixes the named fault.
assertions: The output includes Weak, Fault, Better, Why, and Rubric sections.
The output names a concrete broken relation rather than saying only unclear, awkward, or verbose.
The Better version repairs the fault while preserving the intended meaning.
